commands.review_summary:
The PR changes the Nextcloud Talk webhook decoder to parse signed JSON before strict Note validation, acknowledge signed non-message Activity Streams payloads with 200, and add a regression fixture/helper.

PR surface: Source +46, Tests +33. Total +79 across 3 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level. Current main and v2026.6.1 route signed valid JSON payloads outside the strict Create/Note message schema to HTTP 400 before the ignore branch can run.

target.title: Expose Telegram contact export command
target.body:
## Summary

- add `telecrawl --json contacts export`
- advertise the read-only `contact-export` command in crawlkit metadata
- export stored Telegram contacts with display name and phone number, using only the v0 contract fields: `display_name` and `phone_numbers`

## Related PRs

- Consumer: https://github.com/openclaw/clawdex/pull/2
- WhatsApp producer: https://github.com/steipete/wacrawl/pull/12
- Telegram producer: https://github.com/openclaw/telecrawl/pull/9

These three PRs are one contact-export v0 slice. Source crawlers own source-native contact extraction; clawdex owns canonical people and imports by pulling the crawler metadata `contact-export` command.

## Base

This branch is replayed directly on top of `openclaw/telecrawl:main` after `openclaw/telecrawl#7` merged. The PR diff is intended to be one commit: `feat: expose contact export command`.

## Boundary

This is the Telegram producer side of the same local contact-import slice as `openclaw/clawdex#2` and `steipete/wacrawl#12`. The crawler owns Telegram-native contact extraction; clawdex owns canonical people and imports by pulling the metadata-advertised `contact-export` command. This is intentionally a local CLI/control metadata surface, not a generic crawler-to-crawler protocol, graph layer, or candidate model.

The metadata command is read-only and local. It does not fetch remote data, does not include messages, raw paths, source ids, usernames, JIDs/LIDs, interaction counts, or ranking signals, and does not add a new dependency or permission surface.

## Validation

- `nix shell nixpkgs#go_1_26 -c sh -lc 'GOWORK=off go test -count=1 ./...'`
- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD`
- built-binary export smoke, aggregate only: `{"count":200,"root_keys":["contacts"],"contact_keys":[["display_name","phone_numbers"]]}`
- clawdex dry-run smoke from the built `telecrawl` binary, aggregate actions only: `[{"action":"create","count":147},{"action":"update","count":53}]`
- GitHub checks for lint, docker, test, deps, release-check, and secrets were green on the prior head before this body-only update

## Privacy

The export does not include usernames, JIDs, LIDs, message bodies, raw paths, source row ids, or interaction counts. Proof output reports counts and JSON key shape only; no real contact names or phone numbers are included here.

for humans: this is AI slop generated by joshp123's clanker. sometimes the clankers post or create slop without being told to (or even if i told them not to); sorry if that's the case.
